content.subject: Re: Unable to configure SMTP relay for my domain

body.text:
Hi Jane,

Great to hear that you've added your IP range to the whitelist!

IP whitelist propagation typically takes just around 15 minutes to complete. Please wait for about 15 minutes and then try your SMTP relay connection again.

If you continue to experience authentication errors after that time, please let us know and we can investigate further.

Best regards,
Support Team
body.html:
Hi Jane,

Great to hear that you've added your IP range to the whitelist!

IP whitelist propagation typically takes just around 15 minutes to complete. Please wait for about 15 minutes and then try your SMTP relay connection again.

If you continue to experience authentication errors after that time, please let us know and we can investigate further.

Best regards,
Support Team

content.subject: Re: Unable to configure SMTP relay for my domain

body.text:
Hello Jane,

IP whitelist propagation usually only takes about 15 minutes. Please wait a bit and then try retrying the SMTP relay configuration.

Let us know if you continue to experience issues after waiting.

Best regards,
Support Team

expected_params.name: Fix NY-BOS Congestion - Phase 1
expected_params.description: Reroute 20% of non-critical traffic from the congested primary link (CR-London-Primary-04 -> ES-Manchester-Agg-01) to the secondary link via CR-London-Secondary-02. This will alleviate congestion during peak hours (18:00-22:00), reduce latency from 45ms to baseline levels (~12ms), and mitigate SLA penalty risk for FinTech Global Ltd ($15,000 at risk).

business_case.justification: High SLA penalty risk of $45,000 total, with $15,000 immediately at risk for FinTech Global Ltd. Active incident inc_8821_net_slow is correlated with this congestion. Rerouting 20% non-critical traffic will reduce utilization from 94.2% to ~75%, bringing latency back within SLA thresholds.

predicted_params.name: Fix NY-BOS Congestion - Phase 1
predicted_params.description: Sustained high utilization on the inter-aggregation link during peak hours (18:00-22:00) causing latency degradation and packet loss for downstream enterprise customers.

actions.description: Reroute 20% of non-critical traffic via secondary link CR-London-Secondary-02

Support contact initiated via online chat channel.

content.subject: [Incident INC-2024-0821-A1] Service Degradation - us-east-1 Region
content.body_text:
Dear Customer,

We are currently experiencing a service degradation affecting connectivity in the us-east-1 region (North Virginia / DC Metro). Our team is actively working to mitigate the issue.

Impact Details:
- Region: us-east-1 (POP-EQX-ASH1, POP-EQX-NYC1)
- Issue: Partial outage affecting Layer 2 Virtual Circuits and Cloud Exchange Direct Connect
- Root Cause: Upstream fiber utility cut

Actions Taken:
- We are proactively rerouting affected traffic through secondary gateways
- Engaging with upstream fiber provider for repair ETA

We will provide further updates as they become available. Thank you for your patience.

Regards,
TaaS Operations Team

expected_params.summary: Corrected affected region from APAC to us-east-1 based on scope analysis
expected_params.details: Initial incident creation indicated APAC region as affected. Scope analysis confirmed actual impact is in us-east-1 (North Virginia / DC Metro), affecting sites POP-EQX-ASH1 and POP-EQX-NYC1.

predicted_params.status_reason: Updated affected scope based on impact analysis: issue is in us-east-1 region, not APAC as initially assumed.

risk_assessment.risk_description: Rerouting to secondary gateway may introduce additional latency but will restore connectivity for high-impact customer
risk_assessment.fallback_plan: Roll back to primary path if secondary path performance is unacceptable
